title: enterolobium contortisiliquum
intro: Enterolobium contortisiliquum, also known as the "ironwood" or "monkey ladder," is a species of flowering tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The bark of the Enterolobium contortisiliquum tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Enterolobium contortisiliquum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Enterolobium contortisiliquum are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
